name: The col du Tourmalet from Lourdes
name_original: Le col du Tourmalet à partir de Lourdes
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Lourdes are situated at the foothills of the majestic Pyrenees, providing a dramatic backdrop for cyclists. The region is characterized by significant mountain passes, glacial lakes, and river valleys. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, from the prominent Pic du Jer to the serene Lake Lourdes and the Gave de Pau Valley. This area offers a network of routes through an unspoilt natural environment. Best road cycling routes around Lourdes The most popular road cycling route is The col du Tourmalet from Lourdes, a difficult 61.4 miles (98.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 40 minutes to complete. This route challenges cyclists with a globally recognized climb. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Hautacam – emblematic passes of the Pyrenees, a difficult 40.9 miles (65.8 km) path. This route features a challenging ascent with rewarding views of the Pyrenean landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Small loop of Lourdes, a moderate 23.6 miles (38.0 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 47 minutes. Road cycling around Lourdes is defined by the Pyrenees Mountains, the Gave de Pau Valley, and iconic mountain passes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Lourdes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 180 reviews. More than 12,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Lourdes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Lourdes?
A: Road cycling around Lourdes offers a diverse range of terrain. You'll find routes at the foothills of the majestic Pyrenees, featuring significant mountain passes, serene glacial lakes like Lake Lourdes, and picturesque river valleys such as the Gave de Pau Valley. The area is known for its unspoilt natural environment, with options ranging from challenging climbs to more contemplative rides along greenways.

Q: Are there challenging mountain passes for experienced road cyclists in Lourdes?
A: Yes, Lourdes is an exceptional destination for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge. The region is renowned for its proximity to legendary mountain passes frequently featured in the Tour de France. You can test your endurance on iconic climbs like the Col du Tourmalet, the Col d'Aubisque, Col du Soulor, Col d'Aspin, and Hautacam. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e850102085/the-col-du-tourmalet-from-lourdes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The col du Tourmalet from Lourdes&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 61.4-mile route that challenges cyclists with a globally recognized climb.

Q: Are there easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Lourdes?
A: While Lourdes is famous for its challenging climbs, there are also options for easier rides. The region offers gentle rail trails and quiet, less-frequented roads through picturesque villages. The Gaves Greenway (V81) provides a family-friendly and scenic option, connecting Lourdes to Gavarnie along the Pyrenean foothills. Overall, there are over 7 easy routes available, and many moderate options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e850092368/small-loop of Lourdes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small loop of Lourdes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What landmarks or attractions can I see while road cycling in Lourdes?
A: Many road cycling routes in Lourdes offer views of significant landmarks and natural features. You can often spot the prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5030614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic du Jer&lt;/a&gt;, one of the first foothills of the Pyrenees, or enjoy the serene views around Lake Lourdes. The routes also pass through the Gave de Pau Valley. Additionally, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4489344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lourdes sanctuary estate&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3081717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Lourdes&lt;/a&gt;, depending on your chosen path.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Lourdes?
A: The Pyrenees region, including Lourdes, is generally best for road cycling from late spring through early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is typically more stable, and the higher mountain passes are usually clear of snow. Summer offers warmer temperatures, while spring and autumn provide cooler rides and beautiful seasonal foliage. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Lourdes?
A: Yes, Lourdes offers several circular road cycling routes. These loops allow you to start and finish in the same location, making logistics easier. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e850092368/small-loop of Lourdes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small loop of Lourdes&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 23.6-mile trail. You can also find routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40176451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Voie verte des Gaves – Bridge over the Gave de Pau loop from Lézignan&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a circular experience.

Q: How many road cycling routes are there around Lourdes?
A: There are over 69 road cycling routes around Lourdes documented on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 7 easy, 45 moderate, and 17 difficult options, ensuring there's a ride for every cyclist.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Lourdes?
A: The road cycling routes in Lourdes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 180 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from the challenging mountain passes to the serene valley rides, and the stunning natural landscapes of the Pyrenees. The opportunity to tackle iconic climbs like the Col du Tourmalet is a major draw.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints along the cycling routes in Lourdes?
A: Yes, many routes offer rewarding views, especially those ascending into the Pyrenees. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5030614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic du Jer&lt;/a&gt;, accessible by funicular, provides panoramic views of Lourdes and the surrounding mountains. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064436547/hautacam-emblematic-passes-of-the-pyrenees" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hautacam – emblematic passes of the Pyrenees&lt;/a&gt; are known for their challenging ascents that culminate in spectacular vistas of the Pyrenean landscape.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists in Lourdes?
A: As a popular cycling destination, Lourdes and the surrounding region are generally well-equipped for visitors. While specific parking details vary by route starting point, you can typically find parking in and around the town center. Many accommodations in the area also cater specifically to cyclists, often providing secure bike storage and parking facilities.

Q: Are there cyclist-friendly amenities like cafes or accommodation in Lourdes?
A: Yes, Lourdes is a well-established cycling hub, and you'll find numerous amenities catering to cyclists. Many hotels and guesthouses offer secure bike storage, maintenance areas, and even packed lunches. Along popular routes and in villages, you'll often find cafes and restaurants perfect for a mid-ride break. Local advice on routes and conditions is also readily available from these establishments.

Q: How accessible are the road cycling routes by public transport in Lourdes?
A: Lourdes is well-connected by public transport, making it a convenient base for cyclists. The town has a train station, and local bus services can help you reach various starting points for routes. However, specific public transport options for carrying bicycles can vary, so it's advisable to check with local operators like &lt;a href="https://en.lourdes-infotourisme.com/explore/enjoy-a-wealth-of-outdoor-activities/the-most-stunning-natural-sites-at-the-gates-of-lourdes/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lourdes Infotourisme&lt;/a&gt; for the most up-to-date information on bike-friendly services.
tips:
Bagnères-de-Bigorre is at the foot of the emblematic Pic du Midi. This spa resort, which dates back to antiquity, is located in a peaceful natural setting.

The town is about thirty kilometers from the Col du Tourmalet. The Col d'Aspin, another legend of the Tour de France, is also not far away.

The Col du Tourmalet is one of the most legendary in the Pyrenees and the Tour de France. It is the pass that has been used most often in the history of the Tour since its first time in 1910. Culminating at 2,115 meters above sea level, it is also one of the highest. It is reached from Luz-Saint-Sauveur via 19 kilometers at 7.4% or from Sainte-Marie-de-Campan over 17.2 kilometers at 7.37% on average.

Luz-Saint-Sauveur is a lively and welcoming town in the French Pyrenees. Its strategic location makes it a key crossing point to reach several valleys. Particularly welcoming, the town has no shortage of accommodation and dining options.

waypoints.name: Kilometer Zero of the Tourmalet – Luz-Saint-Sauveur
